meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Project Stall
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement analytics to overcome project stall. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to drive SaaS revenue and validation.
content.answer_block: You aren't failing to finish because of code; you're failing because you're building in the dark. Implementing event-based analytics transforms your 'gut feelings' into actionable revenue data. By tracking user friction points immediately, you identify exactly what to build next, preventing scope creep and ensuring your MVP reaches market-ready status.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is filled with 'perfect' products that nobody actually uses. You’re stuck in the loop of infinite iteration, polishing features that don't move the needle, and wondering why your dev velocity feels like dragging an anchor through concrete. This isn't a lack of discipline; it’s a lack of feedback.

Real founders stop guessing and start measuring. By integrating lightweight, event-driven analytics into your stack, you turn your product into a diagnostic tool. This guide strips away the technical noise, showing you how to wire up your MVP so you can stop building in the dark and start shipping features that actually convert.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Events
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three events that prove user value (e.g., 'signup_complete', 'first_query_sent', 'subscription_initiated').
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current SaaS MVP structure and suggest a minimal schema for tracking user onboarding and core conversion events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'identify the 3 most critical user conversion events for my current project'

steps.title: Inject Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Add a centralized tracking utility to your codebase so you aren't calling third-party APIs directly throughout your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, singleton analytics wrapper in TypeScript that handles event queuing and error suppression for a production SaaS app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'create a centralized analytics middleware file in /lib/analytics.ts'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Flows
steps.description: Place triggers at the exact moments where users drop off. This tells you exactly where your funnel is leaking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my sign-up flow and suggest optimal placement for analytics event trackers to monitor churn points.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'audit the onboarding flow in /src/pages/signup and inject trackEvent calls at each step'

steps.title: Automate Data Visualization
steps.description: Push your events to a dashboard so you can view conversion rates without writing custom SQL queries.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a configuration script to sync my event logs with a lightweight dashboard like PostHog or Mixpanel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure the analytics provider integration using the environment variables in .env'

steps.title: Establish the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Create a weekly ritual where you review event data before deciding on the next sprint's feature set.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a summary prompt that I can use in Claude to analyze my weekly analytics export and suggest the highest-impact feature to build next.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a weekly analytics review template for my dashboard data'

pro_tips: Never track everything; only track events that influence your 'Time to Value' metric.
Use anonymous user IDs before login to track acquisition intent before the user even signs up.
Treat your analytics schema like a database schema—keep it documented and versioned in your repo.
If you can't explain why a metric changed in one sentence, you're tracking too much noise.
Automate your analytics reporting to Slack; if you have to log in to see the data, you won't look at it.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, wait 48 hours for data accumulation, then run your first cohort analysis to identify the exact feature causing user friction.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for your customers. With the right data, you'll stop guessing and start scaling. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
